# wslPath

`wslPath` is a Python module to convert between Windows and POSIX paths in WSL

## Install

From [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/wslPath/):

```python
pip install wslPath
```

From [Conda](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/wslpath):

```python
conda install -c conda-forge wslPath
```

## Usages

```python
import wslPath

# Windows to Posix

## Relative path
pathwin = "hoge\\fuga"
wslPath.to_posix(pathwin)
# -> "hoge/fuga"

## Absolute path
pathwin = "C:\\hoge\\fuga"
wslPath.to_posix(pathwin)
# -> "/mnt/c/hoge/fuga"

# Posix to Windows

## Relative path
pathposix = "hoge/fuga"
wslPath.to_windows(pathposix)
# -> "hoge\\fuga"

## Absolute path
pathposix = "/mnt/c/hoge/fuga"
wslPath.to_windows(pathposix)
# -> "C:\\hoge\\fuga"

# Identify path type (POSIX or Windows)

path = "hoge/fuga"
wslPath.is_posix_path(path)
# -> True

path = "hoge\\fuga"
wslPath.is_posix_path(path)
# -> False

path = "hoge/fuga"
wslPath.is_windows_path(path)
# -> False

path = "hoge\\fuga"
wslPath.is_windows_path(path)
# -> True

```

functions.name: wslPath.main.is_windows_path
documentation: Determine if the given path is in Windows format.

functions.name: wslPath.main.is_posix_path
documentation: Determine if the given path is in POSIX format.

functions.name: wslPath.main.has_invalid_windows_path_chars
documentation: Check if the given path contains invalid Windows path characters.

functions.name: wslPath.main.to_posix
documentation:
Convert a Windows path to a POSIX path
Examples:
    >>> import wslPath
    >>> pathwin = "hoge\fuga"
    >>> wslPath.to_posix(pathwin)
    hoge/fuga

    >>> pathwin = "C:\hoge\fuga"
    >>> wslPath.to_posix(pathwin)
    /mnt/c/hoge/fuga

functions.name: wslPath.main.to_windows
documentation:
Convert a POSIX path to a Windows path
Examples:
\>>> import wslPath
\>>> pathposix = "hoge/fuga"
\>>> wslPath.to_windows(pathposix)
hogefuga

```pycon
>>> pathposix = "/mnt/c/hoge/fuga"
>>> wslPath.to_windows(pathposix)
C:\hoge\fuga
```

functions.name: wslPath.main.wslpath
documentation:
Convert a path to the appropriate format for the current platform.
Examples:
\>>> import wslPath
\>>> # If the current platform is Windows and the path is POSIX, convert to Windows path
\>>> wslPath.wslpath("hoge/fuga")
hogefuga
\>>> # If the current platform is Windows and the path is Windows, return the path as is
\>>> wslPath.wslpath("hogefuga")
hogefuga
\>>> # If the current platform is Linux and the path is Windows, convert to POSIX path
\>>> wslPath.wslpath("hogefuga")
hoge/fuga
\>>> # If the current platform is Linux and the path is POSIX, return the path as is
\>>> wslPath.wslpath("hoge/fuga")
hoge/fuga
